summary: Extract the identical TraceGuard + AccessRecorder + Execute + Record scaffold shared by OCGIncreaseRef, OCDeccreaseRef, and OCReleaseGRefs in object_client_c_wrapper.cpp into one file-local variadic function template (RecordedObjectOp) in an anonymous namespace, rewriting the three extern "C" ops as one-line delegations. Behavior and the C ABI are unchanged; OCPut/OCGet (real data-size metric) and the kv/stream peer wrappers are out of scope. Coverage relies on the existing Go integration tests in go/object/object_client_test.go.

ledger.what: Use a variadic C++ function template (RecordedObjectOp), not a preprocessor macro, for the shared scaffold.
ledger.why: The codebase is C++ and values type/memory safety; a template is type-checked and debuggable while still collapsing each call site to one line. Both were named as candidates in the issue.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose the template and specified it in the spec/plan.

blast_radius.why: File-local helper used by three call sites in a single translation unit; nothing else references it.

reversibility.why: Can be swapped to a macro or inlined again with a local edit to one file.

ledger.revisit_if: A call site needs behavior a template cannot express, e.g. token-pasting to also generate the AccessRecorderKey name.

ledger.what: Hard-code the Record data-size argument to the literal "0" inside the helper and exclude OCPut/OCGet.
ledger.why: All three in-scope ops pass exactly "0" today; parameterizing it would add an unused knob and blur the boundary with OCPut/OCGet, which pass real sizes (std::to_string(cValLen)/std::to_string(totalSize)).
ledger.what_i_did: Baked "0" into the helper; scoped the change to the three constant-metric ops only.

blast_radius.why: Only the three targeted ops are affected; Put/Get and peers are untouched.

reversibility.why: The "0" constant can later be promoted to a helper parameter without disturbing callers.

ledger.revisit_if: A fourth constant-metric op needs a different constant, or OCPut/OCGet are folded into the same helper.

ledger.what: Keep the helper file-local (anonymous namespace in object_client_c_wrapper.cpp); do not generalize into shared util.h across kv/object/stream now.
ledger.why: The issue's requested change targets the object wrapper's three call sites; promoting to a shared header touches kv/stream translation units and their differing metric/return patterns, a broader refactor best done separately.
ledger.what_i_did: Scoped to this file and recorded the peer duplication as a follow-up.

blast_radius.why: No peer files are modified; the deferral affects only future work, not this change.

reversibility.why: The template can be moved into util.h when a consolidation task is undertaken.

ledger.revisit_if: A follow-up task consolidates the kv/object/stream public-op wrappers.

ledger.what: The three ops are extern "C" and rewriting only their bodies to call an internal C++ template leaves their public signatures and linkage unchanged.
ledger.why: The helper is invoked inside each extern "C" function; templates are a C++ implementation detail invisible to the C ABI, so Go/C callers are unaffected.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified object_client_c_wrapper.h declares all three inside 'extern "C" {' (lines 26, 142, 163, 174) and left the header untouched.

blast_radius.why: ABI is preserved, so no downstream caller changes.

reversibility.why: Body-only edit in one file can be reverted trivially.

ledger.revisit_if: The public header signatures of these ops change.

ledger.what: Each OCExecute* worker takes datasystem::RequestParam * as its final parameter, so one variadic template can forward the caller's args and append &reqParam last.
ledger.why: This uniform trailing parameter is what lets a single helper fit all three call sites.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified the signatures: OCExecuteGIncreaseRef (lines 142-145), OCExecuteGDecreaseRef (190-193), OCExecuteReleaseGRefs (238-239) all end with datasystem::RequestParam *reqParam.

blast_radius.why: Only the three call sites depend on this parameter ordering.

reversibility.why: If a worker changes, that single call site can revert to an inline body.

ledger.revisit_if: A worker's parameter order changes so reqParam is no longer last.

ledger.what: Existing Go integration tests already cover the three ops, so no new test is added for this pure structural refactor.
ledger.why: Simplicity First / Surgical Changes: identical-behavior refactors should lean on existing coverage rather than add redundant tests.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified go/object/object_client_test.go exercises GIncreaseRef (lines 114, 162), GDecreaseRef (137, 185), and ReleaseGRefs (TestObjectReleaseGRefs, line 199) through the C API.

blast_radius.why: Test strategy choice; does not alter product code paths.

reversibility.why: A targeted C++ unit test can be added later if reviewers require it.

ledger.revisit_if: Reviewers require a dedicated C++ unit test, or the Go tests do not run in CI for this change.
